name: Appleway
description: A modern florist website for Appleway Florist & Greenhouse in Spokane Valley, Washington, rebuilding the outdated applewayflorist.net informational site using content and assets from the reference site where applicable. Preserve the business's legacy and family-owned history since 1952, but replace the obsolete table-based, spacer-GIF, fixed-width design with refined responsive layouts and updated visuals.
personaStyleGuide: Design the site as a contemporary floral marque that treats legacy as a visual asset, not a nostalgic constraint. The visual anchor is botanical editorialism: oversized serif headlines, immersive floral imagery, and airy compositions that feel like a greenhouse catalog reimagined for a premium modern web experience. Use a high-contrast serif for headings to convey heritage and permanence, paired with a restrained sans serif for body copy and utility text. Favor full-bleed image moments, asymmetry, generous negative space, and cardless content bands instead of boxed modules. Spacing should feel breathable and gallery-like, with large vertical rhythm and deliberate text-image offsets. Color should pull from fresh stem greens, petal blush, warm cream paper, and deep soil-toned text to reflect florist craft and greenhouse warmth without becoming rustic. Shadows should be minimal and soft; depth should come from layering, scale, and overlapping organic shapes rather than UI chrome. Avoid generic SaaS grids, default cards, and dashboard patterns. Replace the obsolete table-based, spacer-GIF, fixed-width feeling with fluid responsive sections, elegant typography as art, and image-led storytelling that honors a family-owned business established in 1952.
features: Updated visuals using content and assets from the reference site where applicable
Preservation of the family-owned history since 1952
Modern replacement for the outdated fixed-width design
Refined florist and greenhouse brand presentation for Spokane Valley, Washington

sections.title: Appleway Florist & Greenhouse
sections.description: A striking responsive hero that introduces Appleway Florist & Greenhouse in Spokane Valley, Washington, using the strongest available floral or greenhouse imagery from the reference site where applicable and immediately signaling that the business is established, trusted, and modernized.
sections.layoutHint: Full-bleed hero with edge-to-edge imagery, oversized serif typography, layered botanical shapes, and an offset text block that breaks the old fixed-width feel.

sections.title: Family-Owned Since 1952
sections.description: A dedicated legacy section preserving the business's family-owned history since 1952, presented as a refined narrative centerpiece rather than a small sidebar note.
sections.layoutHint: Editorial storytelling layout with large date treatment, spacious text composition, and archival-style image framing integrated into a fluid responsive composition.

sections.title: Florist & Greenhouse Information
sections.description: A modern informational content section rebuilt from the reference site, carrying over applicable business copy and assets while translating outdated table-based presentation into clear, elegant responsive layouts.
sections.layoutHint: Cardless open layout with alternating text and imagery bands, strong typographic dividers, and generous spacing that lets floral content breathe.

branding.aesthetic: Botanical editorial luxury with family-owned authenticity

imagery.style: Lush natural-light floral photography with greenhouse atmosphere, soft editorial cropping, and tactile close-ups of blooms, leaves, stems, and shop detail
